All right, welcome to your client tracker for your online fulfillment. So this tracker is super simple and I'm going to show you how to use it and how to add in new members as you sign them up and how to put all of their programming in. So pretty simple. When you get started, these will be blank just like the bottom two are. So when you want to add in a new person, all you are going to do is copy. So you can select all of these rows and you can hit command C or control C and you're just going to drop them right below. Just like that. Then all you want to do is put the new name in there and it can say Bob. Okay, now you have two tabs underneath. One is for Monday, Wednesday, Friday clients and the other is for Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday clients. You can break them up in between coaches if you have coaches doing them, but this is designed for just one person to do them and to kind of split the load so you're not messaging people or you don't have everybody that you're messaging on the same days. So as you guys know from watching the fulfillment, you are going to be reaching out to them via Voxer on Monday, Wednesday, Friday or Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday, three days a week. We are making proactive reach outs. And so these people are the ones that you are going to be reaching out to on Monday, Wednesday, Friday. That way when you get into this sheet you have or let's say it's Monday morning, you know every single person right in front of you that you need to send a message to on Voxer. And then on Tuesday, you click on the other tab and now you have everybody that you need to message on Tuesday. And same thing with Wednesday, Thursday and Friday. So pretty simple. Now let's work our way from the left to the right. So from the left you'll see that we have the top one which is check-ins. So this is us tracking how many times we are checking in with them. So first thing it'll have is the meeting day. So you'll be meeting with your clients every two weeks at the gym. And so this is it would make a lot of sense for that to be a recurring meeting. However, even if it can't be a recurring meeting, this is where you put the most updated meeting time that you set with them. So it's nice if it let's say it's Saturday at 2pm. It would be awesome if you just kept it Saturday at 2pm. If you need to update it, you can just type in the updated meeting time there. So you always keep track. Maybe a nice one, a nice to have would be like, let's say it's not always going to be recurring. You could put the date. So like 5.31 at 2pm. If you did that, then you know it's more specific. But if it's always going to be the same day and time, you could leave it say Saturday at 2pm. The next thing that we want to take a look at is the boxer chat. So the boxer chat is going to be the URL for their chat specifically. That way you don't have to go through a whole thread of 70, you know, messages in boxer to find theirs really quick and send them a message. So to do that is pretty simple. Let's delete this one. Let's say we wanted to add somebody so you're going to open up boxer on your computer. And when you take a look at different boxer threads, as an example, you could just copy this one, hit command C, go back into your sheet. And then right here, you just hit command V or control V if you're on a PC. And now when you click on this, it will open up that chat specifically. Okay. So that's all you do here. Pretty simple. These next three boxes are when you reached out to them, Monday, Wednesday and Friday. Okay. And you'll want to do this. You'll want to check these off each week so you don't forget. And as soon as you reach out to them, put the check box in there. Okay. As soon as you reach out to them, put the check box in there. At the end of the week, if you need to uncheck everybody at once, all you do is highlight all of these and you just hit the space bar. When you do that, it unchecks everything at once. If you want to check them all at once, you just hit the space bar. Okay. So that's how the check-in works. It's a reminder to yourself that you did reach out to them. So they're sending you their scale picks every single day, right? But you don't have to respond to all of those scale picks. You want to respond to three of them or you want to reach out to them, even if it's not a direct response to that picture, you want to reach out to them three days a week so you're being proactive. So that's what the check-in area is for, is that communication and then personally when you're going to meet with them at the gym. The next area is the protocol that they're following. Okay. So that's where it'll say, on your sheet, it'll say curriculum. So the curriculum that they're following. And we have their weight that they're tracking and sending into you. We have their fitness, their nutrition, their supplement regimen, and their progress picks. So weight, this is going to be tracked every two weeks. Now they're sending you pictures of their weight, but that's mostly for accountability purposes. The numbers you're putting in here is all from the in-person meetups. So week zero is going to be nutrition orientation day, their first day. You're putting in their starting weights. Then week two, obviously it's two weeks after. Week four is going to be four weeks after, which should double as like your abandoned hope meeting where you convert them. So they're three weeks finished and now you're having that conversation with them. And then week six would be like way out there. And they should be continuing on to however long you hold on to them. So these are all weights that you're taking on your in-body or whatever you use when they get to your gym and you're entering them in here so you can track them. Next would be the fitness protocol. So let's say the first two weeks you're starting them at 5,000 steps or 3,000 or 10,000, whatever it is, this is for you to know what they're doing, where they should be at. Then week four, you're escalating it to more steps. Week six, you're escalating it again to more steps. So this or whatever your programming is for fitness, that's where you're typing it in right here. Nutrition, again, same thing. Maybe week zero, they're starting off just with a food list and they just eat off the list. Right? Then week two, they are eating the same food list but it's templated. You're telling them a handful of protein. Then maybe week four, it is still templated and they are tracking the food mass. So weighing it and then tracking it in like my fitness pal. And then week six, you could give them new foods and having them track it calorie wise in my fitness pal as well. So this area is where you put the nutrition protocol. So it's shaded a little bit darker because this represents the first six weeks. Then the program changes a little bit, which you'll learn about for the next six weeks. And then it goes into more of a maintenance or we'll talk about it recovery over the past or the final week 16 and on. So the supplement regimen is this is where you're putting in what kind of supplements they're taking. That way when you text them or you meet with them, you know exactly what they're on. So ultimate, ultimate, ultimate, ultimate, maybe down here it switches to a four pack, whatever. Okay. So that is how that works. And then the progress picture would be either pictures that you take at the gym or you can have them send you progress pictures, which probably makes more sense because the meetings are going to be shorter and they will probably give you better pictures if you let them take it themselves and they send it to you over boxer. So that's what I would recommend is let's say this is, let's say that's empty, right? And they send you a picture on boxers. So let's find a picture on boxer. It could be any picture. Okay. So let's say here's a picture. You could just save. Whoops. So you just hit copy image address just like that. And now you go back into your sheet and then you will paste this in here using command paste or command V or now when you scroll over this and you click on this, it is going to just pull up that picture. So you always have it right then and there and it's not going to get deleted or go anywhere unless you delete the thread. So that is how the pictures work. So I would have them send you their front side and back pictures every six weeks. So, and then if you want to do it more frequently, of course you can do it more frequently, but this is what we recommend, especially because in the six week challenge protocol, there's typically before and after pictures and that would fit nicely six weeks in between. So that is basically it. That is how you use this entire sheet. I showed an example underneath of somebody that maybe went a little bit longer or tracking their weight as the weight continues to go down. You'll see the protocol changes after week six. So now they're going into sessions and doing sessions and then the protocol changes for nutrition where they're getting protein and calories and as it is explained, as they come in every two weeks to meet with you at the gym, that's when you're updating their curriculum. You're giving them new nutrition, new fitness things to do, accomplish maybe a different supplement routine. So everything gets updated on these sheets, but it's very simple to fulfill. You just have to make sure that you're tracking everything. So you are always up to date. Again, the Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday sheet is the exact same. You mark off your X's when you've reached out to them. And then finally the curriculum and the timeline. This is just the segments, the phases broken up. So phase one, two and three, which is dedicated fat loss for phase one. Phase two is fat loss and performance and phase three is recovery. And this breaks down exactly what those look like and why the programming is how it is in detail. So this is how you track everything. All three of these sheets will be included on your tracking sheet and you will be able to start adding your clients into it. One tip if you end up having a lot of clients on this sheet, an easy way to find the person that you're looking for is you can just hit command F, right? And then you can type in any name. So if I wanted to type in Bob, it's going to pull up Bob right away. Okay, so if you have more clients, that's an easy way to search through a sheet that might have a lot of names on it. So hopefully you guys enjoyed this and you can go ahead and execute.
